history.qwen_observation: Compilation fails with 'cannot find symbol class Department$' after Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade, indicating the JPA metamodel annotation processor is missing or incompatible with Hibernate 6.
history.qwen_rationale: Spring Boot 3 upgrades to Hibernate 6, which uses org.hibernate.orm:hibernate-jpamodelgen. The project relies on generated metamodel classes (Department$, Employee$) but lacks the correct annotation processor in <annotationProcessorPaths>. Adding it via pom_patch before the SB upgrade ensures the classes are generated during the subsequent compile step.

history.qwen_observation: Build failed because hibernate-jpamodelgen 6.4.0.Final is incompatible with Hibernate 5.6.x used by Spring Boot 2.7.11, preventing metamodel class generation.
history.qwen_rationale: Switch hibernate-jpamodelgen to 5.6.15.Final (groupId org.hibernate) to match the Hibernate 5.6.x version managed by Spring Boot 2.7.11, allowing metamodel generation to succeed before the Spring Boot 3 upgrade.

history.qwen_observation: The initial pom_patch step adding hibernate-jpamodelgen to annotationProcessorPaths broke compilation on JDK 17, causing missing metamodel class errors; the project originally compiles cleanly on JDK 17 without this patch.
history.qwen_rationale: Remove the unnecessary and breaking pom_patch step. Start directly with UpgradeSpringBoot_3_3 to handle the required Spring Boot 2.7 -> 3.3 and javax -> jakarta migration under JDK 17, then proceed with the standard Java 21 plugin and build upgrades followed by Java 21 code transforms.

history.qwen_observation: Compilation fails because Hibernate JPA Metamodel classes (Department$, Employee$) are not generated, likely due to missing or misconfigured hibernate-jpamodelgen in annotationProcessorPaths during the Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade.
history.qwen_rationale: Adding hibernate-jpamodelgen explicitly to the maven-compiler-plugin's annotationProcessorPaths via a POM_PATCH step ensures the metamodel generator runs correctly before the Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade changes the Hibernate version and namespace. This resolves the missing symbol errors and allows the subsequent JDK 21 migration steps to proceed.

history.qwen_observation: The pom_patch step injected an incompatible Hibernate 6 annotation processor into a Spring Boot 2.7 (Hibernate 5) project, causing metamodel generation to fail and breaking compilation before the Spring Boot upgrade could execute.
history.qwen_rationale: Removing the unnecessary pom_patch restores the original build configuration, which compiles successfully under JDK 17. The UpgradeSpringBoot_3_3 recipe will then correctly migrate dependencies to Hibernate 6/Jakarta EE, after which the standard Java 21 plugin and build upgrades can proceed without conflict.
